Package global.thalion.ttio.codecs

Class TtioRansNative

java.lang.Object
global.thalion.ttio.codecs.TtioRansNative
public final class TtioRansNative extends Object
JNI bridge to libttio_rans native library.

Thread-safe. Methods marshal Java arrays to C, call the rANS kernels (which dispatch to AVX2/SSE4.1/scalar at runtime), and return results.

Falls back gracefully: if the native library cannot be loaded (UnsatisfiedLinkError on first call to System.loadLibrary), isAvailable() returns false and callers should use the pure-Java implementation in FqzcompNx16Z.

This class is the Java analogue of the Python ctypes wrapper in python/src/ttio/codecs/fqzcomp_nx16_z.py. The JNI C glue lives at native/src/ttio_rans_jni.c; build it with cmake -DTTIO_RANS_BUILD_JNI=ON.

    TtioRansNative.ContextResolver
    Per-symbol context resolver used by decodeBlockStreaming(byte[], int, int[][], int[][], byte[], int, global.thalion.ttio.codecs.TtioRansNative.ContextResolver).

  • Method Details

    • isAvailable

      public static boolean isAvailable()
      Returns:
      true if the native library was loaded successfully and the encodeBlock/decodeBlock/kernelName native methods are callable.

    • encodeBlock

      public static int encodeBlock(byte[] symbols, short[] contexts, int nContexts, int[][] freq, byte[] out, int[] outLen)
      Encode a block of symbols via libttio_rans.
      Parameters:
      symbols - raw symbols (0..255 each, treated as unsigned)
      contexts - parallel context vector (one uint16 per symbol)
      nContexts - total number of contexts (= freq.length)
      freq - freq table, shape [nContexts][256]; entries must sum to T = 4096 per context with no zero-frequency symbols actually used
      out - output buffer (caller-allocated, sized worst-case)
      outLen - in/out single-element array: input is buffer capacity, output is bytes actually written
      Returns:
      TTIO_RANS_OK (0) or negative error code (TTIO_RANS_ERR_PARAM=-1, ERR_ALLOC=-2, ERR_CORRUPT=-3)

    • decodeBlock

      public static int decodeBlock(byte[] compressed, short[] contexts, int nContexts, int[][] freq, int[][] cum, byte[] symbols, int nSymbols)
      Decode a block of symbols via libttio_rans.
      Parameters:
      compressed - encoded bytes (exactly the slice produced by encode; no trailing slack)
      contexts - parallel context vector (one uint16 per output symbol)
      nContexts - total number of contexts
      freq - freq table, shape [nContexts][256]
      cum - cumulative table, shape [nContexts][256], where cum[c][s] = sum_{s'<s} freq[c][s']
      symbols - output buffer, length nSymbols, written in place
      nSymbols - number of symbols to decode
      Returns:
      TTIO_RANS_OK (0) or negative error code

    • kernelName

      public static String kernelName()
      Returns:
      Selected SIMD kernel name: "scalar", "sse4.1", or "avx2". Determined at library-load time by CPUID dispatch in dispatch.c.

    • decodeBlockStreaming

      public static int decodeBlockStreaming(byte[] compressed, int nContexts, int[][] freq, int[][] cum, byte[] symbols, int nSymbols, TtioRansNative.ContextResolver resolver)
      Decode a block via libttio_rans's streaming context API.

      The native library calls back into resolver.resolve(i, prevSym) before decoding each non-padding symbol, so codecs whose context derives from previously-decoded symbols (e.g. M94.Z order-1 cascades) can drive the C decode kernel without materialising the contexts vector up front. Padding positions use ctx=0 (matching the encoder) and skip the callback.

      See TtioRansNative.ContextResolver for the per-call cost reality. Callers should treat this as an opt-in path with a safe pure-Java fallback on rc != 0 or any thrown exception.

      Parameters:
      compressed - encoded body bytes (V2 native body — same layout as decodeBlock(byte[], short[], int, int[][], int[][], byte[], int) input)
      nContexts - number of contexts (= freq.length = cum.length)
      freq - freq table, shape [nContexts][256]
      cum - cumulative table, shape [nContexts][256]
      symbols - output buffer, length >= nSymbols
      nSymbols - number of symbols to decode
      resolver - per-symbol context derivation callback
      Returns:
      TTIO_RANS_OK (0) or negative error code

    • encodeV4

      public static byte[] encodeV4(byte[] qualities, int[] readLengths, int[] flags, int strategyHint, int padCount)
      Encode flat qualities to an M94.Z V4 stream via the native library.

      Mirrors ttio_m94z_v4_encode in native/include/ttio_rans.h. Auto-tunes (CRAM 3.1) when strategyHint == -1; uses the specified preset 0..3 otherwise.

      Parameters:
      qualities - flat Phred bytes (length == sum of readLengths)
      readLengths - per-read quality counts
      flags - per-read SAM flags (Phase 2 strategy 1 ignores these; auto-tune uses bit 4 = SAM_REVERSE_FLAG)
      strategyHint - -1 = auto-tune (default); 0..3 = explicit preset
      padCount - 0..3 (low-2 bits of the V4 flags byte)
      Returns:
      encoded V4 stream
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the native call returns a non-zero rc

    • decodeV4

      public static Object[] decodeV4(byte[] encoded, int numReads, int numQualities, int[] flags)
      Decode an M94.Z V4 stream via the native library.
      Parameters:
      encoded - V4 stream (must start with "M94Z" + version 4)
      numReads - expected read count (decoder pre-allocates lengths array)
      numQualities - expected quality count
      flags - per-read SAM flags
      Returns:
      [qualities[], readLengths[]] as a 2-element Object[]; element 0 is the byte[] of qualities, element 1 is the int[] of recovered read lengths

    • encodeMateInfoV2

      public static byte[] encodeMateInfoV2(int[] mateChromIds, long[] matePositions, int[] templateLengths, short[] ownChromIds, long[] ownPositions)
      Encode a mate triple via libttio_rans (mate_info v2).
      Parameters:
      mateChromIds - int[N] — -1 if RNEXT='*', else id >= 0
      matePositions - long[N] — 0-based POS
      templateLengths - int[N] — signed tlen
      ownChromIds - short[N] — uint16 from L1 chrom_ids; (short)0xFFFF treated as unmapped sentinel
      ownPositions - long[N] — own POS
      Returns:
      encoded inline_v2 blob ready for HDF5 write
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - on native error

    • decodeMateInfoV2

      public static Object[] decodeMateInfoV2(byte[] encoded, short[] ownChromIds, long[] ownPositions, int nRecords)
      Decode an inline_v2 blob via libttio_rans.
      Returns:
      Object[3]: int[] mateChromIds, long[] matePositions, int[] templateLengths
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - on native error

    • encodeRefDiffV2

      public static byte[] encodeRefDiffV2(byte[] sequences, long[] offsets, long[] positions, String[] cigarStrings, byte[] reference, byte[] referenceMd5, String referenceUri, int readsPerSlice)
      Encode a slice of reads via libttio_rans (ref_diff v2).

    • decodeRefDiffV2

      public static Object[] decodeRefDiffV2(byte[] encoded, long[] positions, String[] cigarStrings, byte[] reference, int nReads, long totalBases)
      Decode a refdiff_v2 blob.
      Returns:
      Object[2]: byte[] sequences, long[] offsets

    • encodeNameTokV2Native

      public static byte[] encodeNameTokV2Native(String[] names)
      Encode a list of read names to a NAME_TOKENIZED v2 (codec id 15) blob.
      Parameters:
      names - ASCII read names; UTF-8 marshalling matches the C entry's const char * const * contract
      Returns:
      encoded blob (starts with magic "NTK2")
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - on native error (rc != 0)

    • decodeNameTokV2Native

      public static String[] decodeNameTokV2Native(byte[] blob)
      Decode a NAME_TOKENIZED v2 (codec id 15) blob to read names.
      Parameters:
      blob - encoded blob produced by encodeNameTokV2Native(java.lang.String[])
      Returns:
      recovered read names in original order
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - on native error (rc != 0)
